selected_important_turn.text: I have plenty of water right now, but need at least 1 pack of food for myself.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn shifts the agent from the prior cooperative exchange into an explicitly self-protective stance and asserts a minimum need. That change in tone and content can reduce integrative momentum, invite harder bargaining from the other side, and makes future tradeoffs more zero-sum.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It's factual but blunt: it declares a reservation without offering any integrative trade or leveraging the agent's surplus (water) to create value. Because it follows the opponent's concrete offer to trade water for food, the agent missed a chance to convert their surplus into a mutually better arrangement or to preserve rapport while protecting a minimum.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Keep the protection of your minimum need but stay cooperative and concrete: acknowledge the other's offer, state the minimum as part of a trade proposal (e.g., I need 1 pack, and I'm willing to give X water/firewood in exchange), or ask a clarifying question about flexibility/timing. Framing the constraint as part of a small, specific counter-offer preserves integrative framing and avoids appearing rigid.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — turning the declaration into a concrete exchange would create additional value (use surplus water to trade) instead of locking in a zero-sum stance.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — proposing an explicit trade that recognizes both parties' needs produces a more balanced outcome than a bare minimum claim.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — a cooperative counter-offer reduces friction and makes it easier to close the deal than an uncompromising assertion.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — staying collaborative while protecting needs preserves trust and reduces chances of escalation.

value2reason.Low: We need to stay hydrated during this trip as well as for cleaning purposes
value2reason.Medium: The weather is colder than expected leading to more usage12
value2reason.High: I have a family of 7 among which 4 are growing in much need of food

value2reason.Low: I already have some of these in stock.
value2reason.Medium: I need food to stay alert and healthy, but I also have some stocked up.
value2reason.High: I need it to make fuel for a fire

dialogue.text: I have a family of 7 among of which 4 are in growing stages. Being the first time out with the whole family, we are less prepared and in much need of food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds rough. 😮 I don't mind sharing out the food, I just need firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That's great! Can I offer 2 packages of firewood in exchange for 2 packs of food?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes, that sounds good to me!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: How about water? do you need? I can offer 2 of these for 1 pack of food
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I have plenty of water right now, but need at least 1 pack of food for myself.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’ll give you 1 food pack for 1 firewood, and keep 2 food and 2 firewood for my family. Let’s finalize this split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 1 food pack for myself, but I’m willing to trade 1 water for 1 food if you need more. Let’s make sure we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'll take 2 food and 2 firewood for my family, and give you 1 food and 1 firewood. Let's confirm this split so we can head out.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
